title: The GQL channel factory must be registered after buildModule() because it needs the document-model queue.
content:
## Claim
The Swarm factory is registered at build time, but the GQL factory registration is deferred until after `buildModule()` completes, because it requires the reactor queue (which hosts the document-model resolver).

## Evidence
From the source: "GQL is registered after build because `GqlRequestChannelFactory` needs the queue (for poll timer backpressure), which requires the document model resolver created inside `buildModule()`."

The wiring code shows this explicitly:

```typescript
const module = await builder.buildModule();
const queue = module.reactorModule?.queue;
if (queue) registerGqlFactory(queue);
```

## Why this ordering
- Swarm's factory has no dependency on the queue, so it can be registered up front.
- GQL's poll timer uses the queue to apply backpressure when the reactor is busy.
- Attempting to register GQL before the queue exists would fail.

## Source
Swarm Channel Architecture, "Wiring Into createBrowserReactor" (createSwarmSyncBuilder note).
description: GqlRequestChannelFactory depends on the reactor queue for poll-timer backpressure, and that queue only exists after buildModule() runs.
